x402 turns an HTTP paywall into an invoice your agent can read, pay, and clear inside one request loop.

the flow runs like this: the agent calls a paid endpoint and receives a 402 carrying machine readable payment requirements, meaning amount, asset, chain, and recipient. ampersend checks that quote against your spending policy, signs the USDC transfer on Base, and retries the request with settlement proof attached. the server verifies payment and returns the data.

in this run the loop fired five times across Apollo and Hunter endpoints for $0.1695 total, each settlement leaving a public transaction hash. the log even caught a wasted two cent call where a parameter name got silently ignored, and the agent flagged it, corrected the param, and kept moving.

metering this granular comes free when payment lives at the protocol layer.
